outcome_contract.why: Refunding requires accessing a buried page inside Caryina only; switching payment processors requires redeploying the entire app; no cross-shop payment visibility exists.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: A standalone payment management app (`apps/payment-manager/`) is live on Cloudflare Workers: per-shop processor selection switchable at runtime via UI, cross-portfolio order list with refund issuance, and the Caryina admin refund route migrated to a proxy then removed. **Scope clarification under Option A:** Caryina's Node.js Axerve execution path is retained as a backend service under Option A; "Caryina payment admin code removal" (Phase 5) applies to the user-facing admin UI and the Stripe direct handler only тАФ not the Axerve Node.js route, which remains until Option B (Axerve REST) is adopt

engineering_coverage.applicable: Identical тАФ refund UI in Payment Manager, same design system; proxy is invisible to UI
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Full UI build needed: order list, order detail, refund modal, provider config form. Design system (`@acme/design-system`) available; Tailwind v4 in new app
Must handle: refund in-flight, refund success/failure, provider switch confirmation, masked credential entry, empty order list, loading states

engineering_coverage.applicable: Shared secret between Payment Manager and Caryina for proxy auth needed; Caryina Node.js route still holds Axerve credentials
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Caryina Axerve credentials move to Payment Manager encrypted DB
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Payment Manager holds AES-256-GCM encrypted credentials; rotation procedure: `wrangler secret put PAYMENT_MANAGER_ENCRYPTION_KEY` + re-encrypt endpoint; Caryina RETAINS its Axerve credentials (env vars) for the proxy path under Option A

engineering_coverage.applicable: Axerve refund logs split: Payment Manager logs the outbound call; Caryina logs the SOAP execution
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Single unified log in Payment Manager
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Audit log model (`PaymentConfigAudit`) in Prisma; refund audit trail per order; structured logs for all provider API calls; Caryina proxy call must be logged on both sides

engineering_coverage.applicable: Existing 9 Caryina refund tests remain valid; new proxy integration test needed; Payment Manager unit tests for proxy wrapper
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Caryina SOAP tests must be replaced with REST mocks; new `@acme/axerve` REST tests needed
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Proxy path test (TC: Caryina returns 200 тЖТ Payment Manager returns 200) must be written; credential encryption unit tests needed; proxy failure test (Caryina down тЖТ Payment Manager returns 503) needed

engineering_coverage.applicable: `ShopPaymentConfig`, `ShopProviderCredential`, `PaymentConfigAudit`, `Order`, `Refund` тАФ 5 new additive models; existing `StripeWebhookEvent` model (schema.prisma:197) is RETAINED and REUSED for webhook log тАФ spec's proposed new model must NOT be created. Existing model fields: `id, shop, type, status, lastError, createdAt, updatedAt` тАФ no `payloadJson` field. Webhook log in Payment Manager UI is scoped to: event type, shop, status, lastError, timestamp тАФ no raw payload display in v1 (payload view deferred to v2 or requires adding `payloadJson` field via additive migration).
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: 5 new models only; Caryina must be wired to call `markStripeWebhookEventProcessed/Failed`; `ShopPaymentConfig.webhookEvents` relation in spec is removed тАФ no such relation needed since webhook table is stand-alone; if raw payload view is required in v1, add `payloadJson String?` to existing `StripeWebhookEvent` model via additive migration

engineering_coverage.applicable: One extra network hop (Payment Manager тЖТ Caryina) for Axerve refunds; acceptable for low-frequency admin operation; circuit-breaker pattern needed for Phase 2
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Direct call from Payment Manager; lower latency
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Axerve refunds via proxy: if Caryina is unavailable, Payment Manager must return 503 with clear error (not silent failure); Stripe path is CF Workers native with no extra hop

engineering_coverage.applicable: Phase 1: Payment Manager standalone, Caryina unchanged тАФ no rollback risk; Phase 2: Caryina's `/admin/api/refunds` becomes a unified proxy to Payment Manager for ALL refund types; rollback = revert route to direct implementation
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Phase 1 is larger: must replace SOAP with REST before building refund API
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Phase 1 is safe (additive only); Phase 2 rollback: revert `apps/caryina/src/app/admin/api/refunds/route.ts` to direct Axerve/Stripe dispatch (pre-proxy state); Phase 3 rollback: revert `provider.server.ts` to env-var read; Phase 3 config reads use `PAYMENT_MANAGER_API_URL` env var + `CARYINA_PM_TOKEN` header (bypasses session gate)

stage_payload.decision_summary: The core architectural decision is how to handle Axerve SOAP refunds in a Cloudflare Workers environment. The `@acme/axerve` package uses `await import("soap")` тАФ a dynamic Node.js import that is blocked in CF Workers V8 isolates. Every other aspect of the app (Stripe, Prisma/Neon, session auth, UI) has confirmed CF Workers patterns in the repo. The Axerve approach must be resolved before Phase 1 refund work is scoped.
goals: Caryina processor selection (Axerve / Stripe) switchable at runtime without redeploy (Phase 3). CMP is Stripe-only and hardcodes `"billingProvider": "stripe"` in `apps/cover-me-pretty/shop.json` тАФ runtime provider switching for CMP is out of scope for v1.
Cross-portfolio order list with filtering, search, and refund issuance
Migrate Caryina's `/admin/api/refunds` route to Payment Manager (then remove or proxy from Caryina)
Checkout reconciliation view and webhook event log (reading from existing `StripeWebhookEvent` store; adding Caryina wire-up)
Basic analytics dashboard (revenue, refund rate, failed payment rate)
non_goals: Customer-facing payment UI (stays in each storefront)
New payment provider integrations beyond Axerve and Stripe

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: The app must run in a CF Workers isolate; approaches that don't are non-starters for the Payment Manager app itself
Operator refund capability cannot regress during migration
Phase 1 should be buildable without awaiting third-party API access confirmations
Two separate Axerve code paths (SOAP + REST) vs one is a long-term maintenance concern
Phase 2 proxy introduces a dependency chain; failure must not silently kill refund capability
Existing Axerve tests (9 in Caryina, unit tests in `@acme/axerve`) should not be lost

options_considered.Option: A тАФ SOAP proxy via Caryina
options_considered.Description: Payment Manager calls a **new non-admin route** `POST /api/internal/axerve-refund` in Caryina (outside the `/admin/:path*` cookie gate in `apps/caryina/src/proxy.ts`) authenticated by `CARYINA_INTERNAL_TOKEN` header. Caryina executes the SOAP call via the existing `@acme/axerve` Node.js path. Phase 2: Caryina's legacy `/admin/api/refunds` route becomes a thin proxy to Payment Manager for all refund types тАФ Payment Manager then routes internally (Stripe: native CF Workers; Axerve: forward to Caryina's internal endpoint).
options_considered.Upside: No new REST client to build; works on confirmed-available infrastructure; no third-party API access unknowns
options_considered.Downside: Caryina must stay running as a dependency for Axerve refunds; Payment Manager тЖФ Caryina coupling; adds one network hop for Axerve refunds; Axerve route cannot be removed in Phase 5
options_considered.Key risks: If Caryina is down, Axerve refunds from Payment Manager fail; `CARYINA_INTERNAL_TOKEN` must be set in both apps

options_considered.Option: B тАФ Replace SOAP with Axerve REST in `@acme/axerve`
options_considered.Description: Build a new fetch-based Axerve GestPay HTTP/REST client in `packages/axerve`; remove SOAP dependency; Payment Manager calls REST directly
options_considered.Upside: Clean CF Workers native path; removes `soap` dependency from monorepo; single Axerve code path long-term; Caryina checkout could also migrate eventually
options_considered.Downside: Axerve REST API availability on production account is unknown (must verify with Axerve account team); significant new build work; existing SOAP tests must be replaced; higher risk
options_considered.Key risks: Axerve may require account tier upgrade or separate API key for REST access; timeline blocked on vendor confirmation
options_considered.Viable?: Yes, conditional on REST API access confirmed

options_considered.Description: Build Payment Manager for Stripe only; Caryina retains its own Axerve Node.js refund route; Axerve refunds still via Caryina admin
options_considered.Upside: Fastest path to value for Stripe orders (the higher-volume provider); unblocked build; no Axerve complexity
options_considered.Downside: Axerve refunds remain buried in Caryina admin тАФ the problem is only half-solved; operator still needs two tools; spec goal not achieved
options_considered.Key risks: Axerve work deferred indefinitely if Option C is accepted as permanent
options_considered.Viable?: Partial тАФ acceptable as interim, not as final

chosen_approach.Recommendation: Option A тАФ SOAP proxy via Caryina, with Option B as a planned follow-on upgrade
chosen_approach.The 9 existing Caryina refund tests (`TC: proxy-shape tests for the new public route, and SOAP-execution tests for the new internal Axerve handler.

rejected_approaches: **Option B (Axerve REST native)** тАФ viable long-term but blocked on Axerve REST API access confirmation; deferred to Phase 4 as an optional upgrade once REST credentials are confirmed

**Option C (Stripe-only v1)** тАФ rejected as final state; acceptable as a short-term scope choice if Axerve complexity threatens Phase 1 delivery, but the operator's stated goal requires both providers

sequencing_constraints: No new `PaymentWebhookEvent` model should be created; webhook log uses the existing `StripeWebhookEvent` model via `packages/platform-core/src/stripeWebhookEventStore.ts`; Caryina must wire up `markStripeWebhookEventProcessed/Failed` calls
Session auth fail-open behavior must be changed to fail-closed for payment-manager (deviation from inventory-uploader template)
Session auth and IP allowlist must be built before any other API routes (security prerequisite)
risks_to_carry_forward: Caryina outage breaks Axerve refunds from Payment Manager тАФ mitigated by fallback error messaging; not solvable without Option B
Real-time dual-write checkout write failures must not block checkout тАФ fire-and-forget with async retry or out-of-band sync

areas.label: Refund issuance (Axerve)
areas.current: Operator navigates to Caryina `/admin` тЖТ POST `/admin/api/refunds` тЖТ SOAP call via Node.js route
areas.trigger: Operator needs to issue Axerve refund
areas.future: (1) Operator opens Payment Manager тЖТ order list тЖТ order detail тЖТ "Issue Refund" modal; (2) Payment Manager POST `/api/refunds` тЖТ resolves provider for order = axerve; (3) Payment Manager POST `https://caryina-host/api/internal/axerve-refund` with `CARYINA_INTERNAL_TOKEN` header (this is a **new non-admin route** that must be added to Caryina тАФ outside the `/admin/:path*` matcher in `apps/caryina/src/proxy.ts`); (4) Caryina Node.js route executes SOAP call тЖТ returns result; (5) Payment Manager records refund in `Refund` table тЖТ responds to UI
areas.steady: Caryina's SOAP execution path (`@acme/axerve`) and Node.js runtime requirement are unchanged
areas.seams: `CARYINA_INTERNAL_TOKEN` must be set in both apps; the new `/api/internal/*` route must be protected by header token check only (not cookie); Caryina outage = Axerve refund capability lost from Payment Manager

areas.label: Refund issuance (Stripe)
areas.current: Same Caryina admin page тЖТ Stripe REST call
areas.trigger: Operator needs to issue Stripe refund
areas.future: (1) Operator opens Payment Manager тЖТ "Issue Refund" тЖТ (2) Payment Manager POST `/api/refunds` тЖТ provider = stripe тЖТ calls Stripe REST directly in CF Workers тЖТ records refund тЖТ responds to UI; (3) Caryina admin refund route becomes a thin proxy to Payment Manager for legacy URL compatibility (Phase 2)
areas.steady: Stripe credentials management stays in Payment Manager DB (encrypted); Stripe SDK usage unchanged
areas.seams: Phase 2 proxy in Caryina must be tested before old direct route is removed

areas.label: Provider selection (Caryina)
areas.current: `PAYMENTS_PROVIDER` env var in Caryina process; change requires Caryina redeploy. CMP hardcodes `"billingProvider": "stripe"` in `shop.json` тАФ CMP has no runtime provider switching.
areas.trigger: Operator needs to switch Caryina from Axerve to Stripe (or vice versa)
areas.future: (1) Operator opens Payment Manager тЖТ Shop config тЖТ selects Caryina provider from dropdown тЖТ saves; (2) Payment Manager writes `activeProvider` to `ShopPaymentConfig` in Neon DB; (3) Caryina's `resolveCaryinaPaymentProvider()` calls Payment Manager `/api/internal/shop-config` with `PAYMENT_MANAGER_INTERNAL_TOKEN` header (service-to-service, not session-authenticated) тЖТ reads `activeProvider` at runtime (Phase 3); (4) Response cached in Caryina's KV for 60s (env-var fallback if endpoint unreachable); (5) New checkouts use new provider; in-flight orders complete on prior provider
areas.steady: Old `PAYMENTS_PROVIDER` env var becomes fallback during bootstrap; CMP Stripe-only configuration unchanged and out of scope; checkout idempotency store format unchanged
areas.seams: Phase 3 adds a network call in Caryina's checkout hot path; `/api/internal/shop-config` must be explicitly exempted from Payment Manager's session middleware; cold-cache scenario must be tested

areas.label: Cross-shop order visibility
areas.current: No unified view; each shop's orders are in separate data stores (JSON file, Prisma `RentalOrder`, etc.)
areas.trigger: Operator needs to see all orders
areas.future: (1) Payment Manager real-time dual-write: each shop's checkout event writes order to Payment Manager's `Order` table in Neon; (2) Operator opens Payment Manager тЖТ order list with shop filter тЖТ full cross-portfolio view with search, filter, pagination
areas.steady: Each storefront continues to maintain its own local order state (Caryina idempotency JSON, CMP `RentalOrder`)
areas.seams: Real-time dual-write requires code changes in Caryina checkout path (Phase 1) and CMP checkout path (Phase 4); write failures must not block checkout

areas.label: Checkout reconciliation
areas.current: `checkoutReconciliation.server.ts` runs in Caryina only; not visible to operator outside logs
areas.trigger: Operator needs to see stale in-progress attempts
areas.future: Payment Manager reconciliation view reads stale attempts from Payment Manager's `Order` table (status = in-progress AND age > 15 min); operator can trigger manual reconciliation or release hold
areas.steady: Caryina's own reconciliation continues running as a safety net during migration
areas.seams: Reconciliation view is only as accurate as real-time dual-write; stale attempts from before dual-write is deployed will not appear

areas.label: Webhook event log
areas.current: Stripe webhook events are already persisted in the `StripeWebhookEvent` Prisma model (written by `packages/platform-core/src/stripeWebhookEventStore.ts`; used by CMP webhook handler); Caryina's webhook handler does NOT yet call the store. The existing model has fields: `id, shop, type, status, lastError, createdAt, updatedAt` тАФ no payload storage.
areas.trigger: Operator needs to audit webhook delivery
areas.future: Payment Manager reads from the existing `StripeWebhookEvent` table (shared Neon DB). Caryina wired to call `markStripeWebhookEventProcessed/Failed` in Phase 1. UI shows: event type, shop, status, lastError, timestamp. **No raw payload view in v1** (model lacks `payloadJson`; scope narrowed from spec). If payload view is needed in v1, add `payloadJson String?` to `StripeWebhookEvent` model via additive migration. No new `PaymentWebhookEvent` model needed or permitted.
areas.steady: Stripe webhook verification stays in each app's webhook route; CMP already writes events; only the read view + Caryina wire-up is new
areas.seams: Caryina must call `markStripeWebhookEventProcessed/Failed` before the Payment Manager webhook log shows Caryina events; spec's raw payload view is out of scope for v1 unless `payloadJson` migration is added
